# FSI Language Rules

- Do not use phrases such as "ensures compliance", "guarantees", "will prevent", or "eliminates risk".
- Prefer wording such as "supports compliance with", "helps meet", "required for", and "recommended to".
- Name regulations precisely, for example "FINRA Rule 4511" or "SEC Rule 17a-4".
- Do not imply live API integration when scripts use representative sample data. Use phrasing like "provides a framework for" or "documents the pattern for" instead of "performs", "scans", "captures", or "aggregates".
- All solution READMEs must use status value "Documentation-first scaffold" until tenant binding is implemented.
